questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: Read the text and choose the correct answer. John Lennon. John Winston Lennon was born in Liverpool in 1940. When he was only two years old, his father left the family. Two years later, when John was four, his mother also left, and John went to live with his aunt, Mimi Smith. At the age of sixteen, he started his first band. In 1957, Paul McCartney joined him, and later Paul’s friend George Harrison became the lead guitarist. Pete Best was the group’s first drummer, but after he left, Ringo Starr became the final member. The band became known as the Beatles. In 1960, the group spent four months in Hamburg, Germany. There, they played music for eight hours every night, and this period helped begin Beatlemania. After returning to England, John married Cynthia Powell in 1962. They had a son named Julian. The Beatles made many albums, but their final official concert took place at the end of 1966. In November of that same year, John met Yoko Ono, who later became his second wife. After the Beatles separated in 1970, each member of the band recorded solo albums. In 1971, John wrote “Imagine,” a song that is still famous today. In the 1970s, John moved to the United States. His second son, Sean, was born in 1975. John’s life ended on December 8, 1980, when Mark Chapman, a fan, shot him five times outside his apartment in New York. John Lennon was only forty years old. Where was John Winston Lennon born?

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text:
Read the text and choose the correct answer. Three BC Festivals. In British Columbia, different communities hold festivals to celebrate what makes their towns and cities special. One of these events is the Pacific Rim Whale Festival on Vancouver Island. Every year, around 20,000 grey whales pass along the west coast of the island. They travel from Mexico to the Arctic and then return, making a journey of about 16,000 kilometres. Each March, people living near Pacific Rim National Park celebrate this long whale migration. Because the whales swim close to the shore while moving north, people can watch them from the land or from boats. Another unusual event takes place on the east coast of Vancouver Island. It is the Nanaimo Bathtub Race. In this race, participants travel through the water in boats made from bathtubs. Nanaimo is known for having the oldest and most famous bathtub boat race in the world. The event began in 1967 as a funny local activity, but today the World Championship Bathtub Race is treated as a serious sport and attracts racers and visitors from many countries. Vancouver also has a well-known water festival called the Dragon Boat Festival. Dragon boat racing began in China more than two thousand years ago. It was introduced to Vancouver during Expo 86, the World’s Fair. The festival returned in 1989 as a celebration of the many cultures living together in the city. It begins with a ceremony that awakens the dragon and gives strength to the people and the boats. Each dragon boat has a dragon’s head at the front and a tail at the back. A team of about 20 people sits in the boat and paddles to the rhythm of a drummer, who sits at the front.

How old was John Lennon when his life ended?

Read the text and choose the correct answer. Three BC Festivals. In British Columbia, different communities hold festivals to celebrate what makes their towns and cities special. One of these events is the Pacific Rim Whale Festival on Vancouver Island. Every year, around 20,000 grey whales pass along the west coast of the island. They travel from Mexico to the Arctic and then return, making a journey of about 16,000 kilometres. Each March, people living near Pacific Rim National Park celebrate this long whale migration. Because the whales swim close to the shore while moving north, people can watch them from the land or from boats. Another unusual event takes place on the east coast of Vancouver Island. It is the Nanaimo Bathtub Race. In this race, participants travel through the water in boats made from bathtubs. Nanaimo is known for having the oldest and most famous bathtub boat race in the world. The event began in 1967 as a funny local activity, but today the World Championship Bathtub Race is treated as a serious sport and attracts racers and visitors from many countries. Vancouver also has a well-known water festival called the Dragon Boat Festival. Dragon boat racing began in China more than two thousand years ago. It was introduced to Vancouver during Expo 86, the World’s Fair. The festival returned in 1989 as a celebration of the many cultures living together in the city. It begins with a ceremony that awakens the dragon and gives strength to the people and the boats. Each dragon boat has a dragon’s head at the front and a tail at the back. A team of about 20 people sits in the boat and paddles to the rhythm of a drummer, who sits at the front.

questions.text:
Бухару-жырау принадлежат слова: «Не ввергай себя в бесконечные тяжбы за скот, за землю — скот может погибнуть, земля останется и после тебя. Довольствуйся посланным Богом, подумай о своем достоинстве». 

Какая аналогия наиболее уместна?

payload.text:
Бухару-жырау принадлежат слова: «Не ввергай себя в бесконечные тяжбы за скот, за землю — скот может погибнуть, земля останется и после тебя. Довольствуйся посланным Богом, подумай о своем достоинстве».

options.text: Знай и помни, что судиться порой бесполезно.
Знай и помни: Богу — Богово, кесарю — кесарево.
Знай и помни, что не хлебом единым живет человек.
Знай и помни, что душа и плоть неотделимы.
Знай и помни, какое наследство ты оставишь потомкам.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: Read the text and choose the correct answer. John Lennon. John Winston Lennon was born in Liverpool in 1940. When he was only two years old, his father left the family. Two years later, when John was four, his mother also left, and John went to live with his aunt, Mimi Smith. At the age of sixteen, he started his first band. In 1957, Paul McCartney joined him, and later Paul’s friend George Harrison became the lead guitarist. Pete Best was the group’s first drummer, but after he left, Ringo Starr became the final member. The band became known as the Beatles. In 1960, the group spent four months in Hamburg, Germany. There, they played music for eight hours every night, and this period helped begin Beatlemania. After returning to England, John married Cynthia Powell in 1962. They had a son named Julian. The Beatles made many albums, but their final official concert took place at the end of 1966. In November of that same year, John met Yoko Ono, who later became his second wife. After the Beatles separated in 1970, each member of the band recorded solo albums. In 1971, John wrote “Imagine,” a song that is still famous today. In the 1970s, John moved to the United States. His second son, Sean, was born in 1975. John’s life ended on December 8, 1980, when Mark Chapman, a fan, shot him five times outside his apartment in New York. John Lennon was only forty years old. Where was John Winston Lennon born?

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text:
Read the text and choose the correct answer. Three BC Festivals. In British Columbia, different communities hold festivals to celebrate what makes their towns and cities special. One of these events is the Pacific Rim Whale Festival on Vancouver Island. Every year, around 20,000 grey whales pass along the west coast of the island. They travel from Mexico to the Arctic and then return, making a journey of about 16,000 kilometres. Each March, people living near Pacific Rim National Park celebrate this long whale migration. Because the whales swim close to the shore while moving north, people can watch them from the land or from boats. Another unusual event takes place on the east coast of Vancouver Island. It is the Nanaimo Bathtub Race. In this race, participants travel through the water in boats made from bathtubs. Nanaimo is known for having the oldest and most famous bathtub boat race in the world. The event began in 1967 as a funny local activity, but today the World Championship Bathtub Race is treated as a serious sport and attracts racers and visitors from many countries. Vancouver also has a well-known water festival called the Dragon Boat Festival. Dragon boat racing began in China more than two thousand years ago. It was introduced to Vancouver during Expo 86, the World’s Fair. The festival returned in 1989 as a celebration of the many cultures living together in the city. It begins with a ceremony that awakens the dragon and gives strength to the people and the boats. Each dragon boat has a dragon’s head at the front and a tail at the back. A team of about 20 people sits in the boat and paddles to the rhythm of a drummer, who sits at the front.

How old was John Lennon when his life ended?
